candidates.stmt:
Hi there! I’m Steph, your green candidate for Brookmans Park and Little Heath. I’m a trading strategist by day (working in environmental products), and the co-chair of our local Green Party by night!

I was born and brought up in Brookmans Park, and have spent my adult life appreciating how precious our area is – and wanting to ensure we maintain our beautiful home. I have been fortunate in my education and career, and I really do owe it to the community here in Brookmans Park for having watched out for me growing up, I wouldn’t have had the confidence or ability to step into the big wide world without it. It’s a special place for me, and I’m keen to give back to the people and community however I can.

My career has been dedicated to understanding how we can implement solutions to social and climactic challenges in an economically sound and efficient way. This has included work in Green Bonds, Emissions trading schemes and broader carbon markets (working for FTSE 250 and 100 companies).

My local priorities include:

-Supporting local businesses
-Opportunities for young adults
-Creating a safe & strong community
-Preserving the green belt
-Avoiding poorly planned development
-Cost of living
-Road and infrastructure condition
-Fly tipping, vandalism
-Re-wilding and local nature initiatives (including initiatives to increase opportunities for young people to engage with nature)

Outside of work, I love time spent reading, doing yoga and pilates, running, and generally finding beautiful adventures to go on! Get in touch if you feel like a walk in Gobions, cocktails at the Brookmans, or to join for a yoga session!

I have lived in Brookmans park for over 30 years and know the area very well.  I am now retired but I am still deeply involved in the local community and am the chair of the North Mymms Memorial Hall as well as being a councillor on the North Mymms Parish Council.

I am also a keen leisure cyclist which makes me very aware of the potholes!

I have been a resident of Welwyn Hatfield for 10 years and am very pleased to be standing for the Heritage Party in this year’s elections.

I love my community, but I believe we are in desperate need of change from the old political parties that put vested interests before local people and have let us down many times.

The Heritage Party stands for simple, common-sense values and principles that will restore purpose and prosperity to our nation. We have a great heritage that has been given to us by our forefathers for us to enjoy and pass on to future generations.

Our core principles are free speech and liberty, national sovereignty, low immigration, self-sufficiency in skills and energy, traditional family values and financial responsibility in government. We believe in the preservation of our Green Belt and our places of historic interest.

Local governments must be efficient and accountable. We reject the plan to replace district and county councils with unitary authorities and Sadiq Khan-style county mayors.

We reject the woke ideologies of critical race theory, 100 genders and climate alarmism. Instead, we hold that Britain is a good country, there are two sexes – male and female – determined by anatomy and chromosomes, and we should not replace coal and gas power stations with wind turbines and solar panels that are causing household energy bills to go through the roof and are killing off nature and potential land for agricultural use.

We will repeal climate emergency declarations and scrap net zero locally.

My passion is education and as a local teacher I am deeply invested in delivering for our area and making it the best it can be - for all generations. I always believe we should support every individual with the opportunity to be a valuable member of our community and ensure people have the skills they need to thrive.

I love our town and its unique character and as your Councillor, I want to focus on preserving the beauty of nature in Welwyn Garden City. We are lucky to live in such green and vibrant area and I am committed to protecting our local environment.

Our Community is special and deserves a dedicated voice at Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council and with your support, I will work hard for Haldens ward - listening, acting and standing up for our community.

Hi, I’m Lucy and I’m standing to be re-elected as your Green Party councillor for Haldens on Thursday 7th May.

For the past eight years, it truly has been a privilege to serve as a councillor for Haldens. I am appealing to have your trust again so I can continue supporting the community in what looks to be a busy and difficult upcoming year, starting with the new Local Plan and Local Government Reorganisation.

As your local councillor, I will be able to achieve even more for you, from working closely with the county councillor to fix potholes and grass verges, to pushing for fairer and greener housing policies and building.

I have been utterly dismayed by reports of a lack of care towards the housing of vulnerable persons, whether that be through allocations or repairs. As I stated in the chamber, it is not good enough, and I will continue to push for better.

This election is really clear, it’s either Labour, or you can vote for the Green Party and me on Thursday 7th May to make hope normal again for Haldens and Welwyn Hatfield.

As a Councillor, resident of the ward and parent, I am deeply invested in our area and want to ensure our Garden City remains the best it can be, for residents today and for the next generation. I continue to support residents with issues large and small and always try to resolve local problems. As Lib Dems, I and my fellow Councillors are always active, keeping in touch all year round through regular Handside resident forums, newsletters or talking to you on the doorstep.

As the Executive Member for Housing, I am proud that our award winning social housing energy efficiency scheme is saving residents an average £420 a year and lowering their carbon footprint. as well as many other great initiatives.

I want to continue working hard for the residents of Handside ward - listening, taking action and standing up for our community.

Hi, I’m Caleb and I have lived in Welwyn Hatfield my entire life, I grew up in Handside and attended Stanborough School. I’m an electrician by trade and help run a monthly open mic night at the Hatfield Tap.

I’ve witnessed first hand the damage cuts to local services have caused to our community, so I joined the Green Party to push for more accountability within local councils and to drive positive change for everyone that lives in this beautiful borough.

Hi, my name is Andy and I have lived in Hatfield since 2018 – building a family here with my wife and, more recently, my twin sons.

I have worked in education in Hertfordshire local government since 2022 and I’m standing for the Green Party as an advocate for our community, particularly those who are young or from global majority backgrounds. My sons both attend school in Hatfield and I intend to be a voice for them and other local residents, through a time of great change in our county and a period of national and global instability.

I strongly feel that the Green Party is the progressive choice for Hatfield residents, who hope for a kinder, fairer society.

I am standing as a candidate for the Liberal Democrats in Hatfield Central because I believe in open, fair and community-focused politics. I am committed to representing local residents with respect, promoting equal opportunities, supporting sustainable development, and working constructively to improve local services.

I aim to be a strong liberal voice that listens, engages and serves the community with integrity and transparency.

Hi, I’m Larry and I’m standing to be re-elected as your Green Party councillor for Hatfield East on Thursday 7th May.

I’ve been a borough councillor for four years, working hard to build a better home for us all.

A £70,000 renovation of Millwards Park was achieved under my leadership, helping to make it more accessible for disabled children. A new sensory area was also introduced for those children needing a little bit extra.

I speak up against overdevelopment of our green spaces. Green space is really important, not only for the environment, but our own health and wellbeing.

Your choice in this election is really clear, it’s either Labour and Lib Dems again, or you can vote for me, and the Green Party, on Thursday 7th May! Help change Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council!

I have lived and worked in the area for over 40 years and am embedded in the local community. My daughters and their young families live in Hertfordshire and are part of the wider community of Hertfordshire.

During my 4 years on the Hatfield Town Council I worked on the planning committee to ensure development was in keeping with the surroundings and not over development. I supported the development of the allotments in the town and supported local events for local residents.

I am standing in the local elections for the Borough Council because I want the place I live in to be the best it can be. Hatfield needs a strong voice in the council to push forward policies that enhance our town. There are lots of things about Hatfield to be proud of and recent improvements in the Town Centre show what can be done. Hatfield has a long history and a bright future which we should celebrate.

If elected I will join a growing number of Liberal Democrat councillors who will work hard to make your town a better place to live.

I am standing as a candidate for Liberal Democrats for the South West ward of Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council because I believe our town and Borough deserves honest leadership, practical solutions and a stronger voice in local decision-making. I moved to Hatfield in May 2022 with my family. We weren’t just looking for a place to live- we were looking for a place to belong. We found it here. I felt honoured that my daughter could grow up here and attend local schools.

Living locally means I share and understand the many challenges that local residents face. I decided to stand for the Council as whilst ours is a tight-knit community, it often feels that most political parties care more about national issues than what is happening locally. For me, what you say is important, but what you do even more so and I know that the Liberal Democrat values best represent my own, especially their commitment to personal liberties and freedom of expression.

I strongly believe that more needs to be done to challenge the root causes of litter and fly tipping and that is why, if elected, I will continue to ensure stronger management of Houses of Multiple Occupation (HMOs), supporting the good landlords and challenging those who do not meet their responsibilities.

Hi, my name is Georgia and I’ve been living in Welwyn Hatfield for almost thirty years. I’m standing for the Green Party because I believe caring for people and caring for the environment are two intertwined goals. We need to build a fairer system for everybody while also protecting the planet in order to foster a kinder, more community-oriented world for future generations to enjoy.

Hello, I am Andreas and I have lived locally for 20 years, and in Hatfield since 2017. I work as a bioscience lecturer at the University of Hertfordshire and joined the Green Party in 2020. I am passionate about protecting our environment while supporting secure employment, a free and efficient NHS, social justice, and peaceful international cooperation.

My name is Adam Edwards. I’ve lived in Birchwood since 1989. My kids have benefited hugely from the local schools, the Mid-Herts Music Centre and from the QE2 hospital, where they were both born.  This is my community and I want the very best for our area for me, my family and all my neighbours.

Hatfield needs strong voices to stand up for everyone who lives here. If elected I would hold the council to account and work hard to get the best for the people I represent.

I have been campaigning for the past eight years to see upgraded paths and pavements, benefiting the elderly, parents with pushchairs, people in disabled buggies and those who cycle.  But there is more to do:  there’s no safe cycle route from Birchwood to the schools in Hatfield!  If we are to combat obesity and pollution, we need more people walking and cycling, leaving the roads less congested for those who have to use a car or van. To this end I’ve also been a member of WelHatCyling since the group was founded.  We chase up on faults and problems and I’ve led the group clearing litter from the Alban Way in Hatfield.

My greatest strength may well be my normality. I am a Dad before anything else. I hate injustice and unfairness and I am tenacious in tackling problems. I will always listen to you, try to understand your problems and work alongside you to try to resolve them. I hope I can count on your support.
Hi, I’m Esther and I have lived in Welwyn Hatfield for my whole life. I am standing for the Green Party because they are the best alternative to Reform.

Hi, I’m Brad and I am deeply passionate about caring for the environment and ensuing that the borough, and the UK, can provide positivity for their citizens! I grew up in Welwyn Hatfield and worked for the borough council. I now work for a national charity.

As a younger voter, I see politics at the moment as a vicious battleground where there is a lot of focus on winning and losing. I believe we should be taking every opportunity to work together for the sake of our communities and to make the world a better place.

Hello, my name is Ian. I was born at the QEII in 1964 and apart from three years in Birmingham obtaining a degree in Mechanical Engineering and five years post graduate research in Physics in the US, I have lived my whole life in Welwyn Hatfield. In 1996, working as a scientist, I was lucky enough to be able to afford to buy my own home, something which has become increasingly difficult these days. I believe that there is an urgent need to provide more affordable housing, ideally with an investment in new council homes.

I have been an environmental and anti-war activist since the late 1980s, particularly concerned about the effects of climate change, and getting involved with the Camps for Climate Action in the late 2000s. However, I had never been a member of political party until I joined the Green Party in 2014.

Locally, I have focused on active travel and sustainable transport, particularly on improving conditions for cycling. I helped set up the Hertfordshire Cycling Campaign and I am deputy chair and cycling representative on the Hertfordshire Local Access Forum, a statutory body concerned with access to the countryside.

I have been a self-employed gardner since 2010 and for leisure I enjoy going to festivals and walking in the mountains.

I’ve made Panshanger my home since 2023, drawn to its green spaces, Garden City heritage, and the sense of fellowship people share here. Getting to know neighbours and becoming part of local life has shaped how I see the community.

My work and volunteering have taught me to listen, understand how places function, and notice the daily challenges people face. From making buildings safer and more sustainable to helping in emergencies with the ambulance service, these roles have given me a practical outlook and a strong belief in caring for our area and everyone in it.

Helping communities grow is what drives me. I care about Panshanger’s character, the well-being of everyone who lives here, and the future of the place I now call home. I hope to use my experience, my ties to the area, and what I’ve learned from talking with residents to make a positive difference, bring people together, and support good decisions for our neighbourhood.

Hi, I’m Alice and I have lived in Welwyn Hatfield my whole life. I am standing for the Green Party because I hope to stop Reform and further cuts to vital local services, especially NHS services and benefits and support for those in need.

I believe a Labour run County Council can truly improve the lives of Hertfordshire residents. As your County Councillor, I want to be part of that.

I can represent residents effectively if elected to the county council. I will listen to them, understand their needs, and actively pursue their cases. I am committed to making a positive impact on the local area.

As a fellow Peartree resident, I am deeply invested in delivering for our area and making it the best it can be for the residents of today and for future generations. With Local Government Reorganisation on the horizon, I want to stand up for our community by campaigning for the creation of a Welwyn Garden City Town Council. I want to continue tackling multiple housing issues, address parking problems alongside my fellow Peartree Councillors and address all the multitude of other issues residents bring to me.

In the last four years, I have played a substantial part in ensuring the Councils finances are on a secure footing as Cabinet member for Resources and more recently as the shadow for that position.

With your continued support, I will carry on working hard for Peartree Ward - listening, acting and standing up for our community.

Hi, I'm Tanya and I'm standing to be elected as your Green Party councillor for Peartree on Thursday 7th May.

As a Peartree resident, I put Green values into practice every day. I founded CultureWood CIC, a social enterprise where we are passionate about nature connection, running forest school programmes for local schools and for the wider community, working with people of all ages. I lead a local community garden group at King George V, where we are literally digging in to protect our environment, and are creating a community and green space where we can learn, share, grow food, build resilience and improve the park for nature.

I'm standing because our ward needs a councillor who will fight just as hard to protect our green spaces, tackle the cost of living crisis, and ensure the council acts on its climate promises.

Your choice in this election is really clear, it’s either the Lib Dems again, or you can vote for me and the Green Party on Thursday 7th May and help change Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council!

As a Councillor, resident, parent and member of both the Orchard Tennis Club and WGC Cricket Club, I am deeply invested in our area.  I want to ensure our Garden City remains the best it can be for residents today and for the next generation. I continue to support residents with issues large and small from repairing verge damage to defending vulnerable residents with critical housing repairs.

As a Liberal Democrat group, we have kick-started the WGC Town Council process, supported our environment and our precious chalk streams and helped introduce parking permits for voluntary carers.

I will fight hard as your Councillor and use my experience to shape positive changes for the benefit of Sherrards and Welwyn Garden City residents.

Hi, I’m Phil and I have lived in Welwyn Garden City with my family for 20 years. I work as an earth scientist at a London university and am passionate about environmental education and inclusive access to the outdoors. I joined the Green Party in 2020 and have met a wonderful group of like-minded people who are working hard to spread the Green message of hope for people and planet.

**It is my pleasure to represent Welham Green and Hatfield South at Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council, Hatfield South at Hatfield Town Council and as the Executive member for Environment at Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council and Deputy leader of the Council.**

Since being elected in 2022, I’ve been working hard to be a voice for the community both in and out of of the Council chamber. Welham Green and Hatfield South is a diverse community with many concerns and aspirations for what they want from their Council. Me and my colleagues Paul and Adrienne are doing what we can to make Welham Green and Hatfield South the best place to live and work. This involves dealing with issues that affect our residents. Including Parking, Fly tipping and maintaining our Green spaces.

In May 2023, I was given the privilege of becoming the Executive member for Environment. This has given us the opportunity to fix the appalling state of grass cutting in our borough, neglected by the previous Conservative administration. My other priorities for this position will be maintaining a high quality of waste collection both residentially and at recycling centres as well as combating fly tipping.

As Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council’s youngest Councillor as well as a former student of the University of Hertfordshire, I do my best to represent members of the community that often feel neglected by modern Politics. I also run the Welham Green friendship group, a monthly get together setting to combat isolation in older members of our community, which is even more important in a post-Covid world.

Hi, I’m Joshua and I’m a lifelong resident of Welham Green. I believe in everyone getting a fair shot regardless of their background. I’m standing for the Green Party because I believe they’re the antidote to the worst of British politics, and they seek to put people and community over profit and capital.

Hello, I am William and I have lived in Welwyn Hatfield for nearly 20 years and have been a member of the Green Party for 35 years.

Now is the time to vote Green, as I strongly feel that we are the progressive choice for Welwyn Hatfield residents with policies that propose more innovative solutions at a local level to deal with long standing problems such as the cost of living and climate change.

We are the party for voters who want a kinder, fairer society.

Hi, I’m Andrew and I joined the Green Party as I believe our politics needs to be different. I believe in a politics that shares power more evenly across our society and prioritises our climate more than wealth creation for the few.

I moved with our family from Welwyn Garden City to Welwyn in 1965, where my wife became a teacher, and our children attended local schools. I worked in medicines research until I left to start my own IT and sports consultancy company in 1996. I have managed a youth sports club since 1996, and over several years have raised Sport England funds in the borough for example, for Gosling Sports Park improvements, young school leaders’ projects, and older people’s exercise. As well as concerns for the environment

I am a member of the Royal Society of Biology, the British Computer Society, Herts and Middlesex Wildlife Trust, and The National Trust.

I have always been passionate about maximising health opportunities for everyone and feel there is a lot more to be done in Welwyn and the borough.

I am a married 50 year old accountant from the Northern part of Welwyn Garden City and my family and I have lived here for almost 20 years, so I know the area well.

I have been a member of the Liberal Democrats for several years now after realising that the policies of the two main parties no longer align with my views and what is important to me.

Hello, I'm David and I'm standing to be elected as your Green Party councillor for Welwyn West on Thursday 7th May.

As a parish councillor, I have worked closely with the Digswell Residents’ Association to increase and also enhance the quality of Welwyn’s green areas. I have assisted the DRA with:

-Opening up Digswell Orchard for residents

-Taking over the ownership and maintainence of the Wildings Nature Reserve from the RSPB to make it available for residents’ enjoyment and tranquility

-Preserving the historical nature of Digswell Village with our first and much loved village sign

-Maintaining the playing fields and riverside pathway.

I have also provided financial and legal assistance for the Singlers Marsh Action Group to attain Village Green status for Singlers Marsh. We are working to create a new skateboard park for the health and enjoyment of the youthful members of our community.

As your local councillor, I will work closely with the county councillor to fix potholes and grass verges, and will push for fairer and greener housing policies and building.

Your choice in this election is really clear, it’s another Conservative, or you can vote for me and the Green Party on Thursday 7th May and help change Welwyn Hatfield Borough Council!
I believe that something has to change in our politics today and the Liberal Democrats are the only party that can do that. I am standing in Welwyn West to help bring about that change.
